body.title: [sprint] Node merge/dedup discipline (living entities) so repeated misses merge not duplicate
body.intention: I intend that concept-nodes are living entities that merge, so the graph stays coherent as misses accumulate.
body.description:
**Problem:** teach-on-miss writes nodes repeatedly; without merge the graph bloats and overlap-similarity fragments.
**Proposed shape:** When a pushed/created node overlaps an existing concept, MERGE (compete/merge/decompose, the intentions discipline one level down) rather than duplicate. Define the merge key + conflict handling.
**Affected files:** word_graph node write/merge path.
**Design rules:** living-entities discipline; log merges.
**Scope boundary:** node merge/dedup only.
**Completion criteria:** two near-identical concept pushes resolve to ONE node; test asserts no duplicate concept nodes after repeated similar misses. Haiku-buildable once merge key is defined.
**Test plan:** unit: push concept twice (slight variation) -> single merged node; distinct concepts stay separate.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)

[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)

[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
